headline: TE Connectivity Weighs Geopolitical Risks And Pricing Power After Earnings Beat
summary: TE Connectivity (NYSE:TEL) reported fiscal Q2 results that exceeded analyst estimates. CEO Terrence Curtin highlighted that ongoing conflict in the Middle East could disrupt supply chains and lift material costs. The company may pass higher input costs on to customers through potential price increases. TE Connectivity, a major supplier of connectors and sensors for automotive, industrial and communications applications, now has more than just earnings to watch. The company sits at the heart...
